title: agentbox manifest
description: JSON Schema for agentbox.toml — the manifest-driven configuration for the agentbox container.

federation.description: DEPRECATED: Use [mesh] instead. This section is retained for backwards compatibility with old manifests only.

mode.description: Deployment shape: standalone (all local fallbacks) or client (federated with host mesh). DEPRECATED: set mesh.mode instead.

external_url.description: Base URL of the host mesh. Required when mode=client. DEPRECATED: set mesh.external_url instead.

adapters.description: Five-slot adapter configuration. Each slot resolves to one of three implementation classes.

pods.description: Durable linked-data storage adapter. local-solid-rs is the first-class first-party implementation (ADR-010); external federates with a host-provided Solid server; off returns AdapterDisabled. The legacy local-jss Python stub was removed in 2026-04-25 — operators with old manifests must flip to local-solid-rs and add the [security.exceptions.solid-pod-rs] writable-volume block.

memory.description: Vector memory adapter. Default (and recommended) value is external-pg backed by ruvector-postgres. embedded-ruvector is a per-session cache only and is not a durable source of truth.

orchestrator.description: Agent spawn and monitor channel adapter.

enabled.description: Enable desktop environment.

stack.description: Desktop stack selection. Default is x11-openbox. hyprland-wayland is available but not the recommended default for containerised deployments.

webgpu.description: WebGPU flag for desktop apps that use GPU.

metrics_port.description: Port for the Prometheus /metrics endpoint. Default 9091.

otlp_endpoint.description: OpenTelemetry OTLP exporter endpoint. Empty = traces dropped.

providers.description: API-provider credential gates. Each key is a provider name. Known provider keys include: anthropic, openai, gemini, zai, perplexity, deepseek, ollama, xinference. ollama does not require an API key — set enabled=true and sidecar=true; env_var may be omitted (W040). xinference is similar: set sidecar=true and supply an optional_env_vars base-URL override.

enabled.description: Whether this provider is active. When true, env_var must be set in the environment (E017).

env_var.description: Name of the environment variable holding the primary API key or token.

optional_env_vars.description: Names of optional supplementary env vars (e.g. base URL overrides).

auth_mode.description: Credential model. "api_key" requires env_var to be set at boot (E017). "oauth" defers auth to an in-container CLI flow (e.g. `claude login`, `codex login`, `claude-zai login`) and skips the env-var check. Only currently honoured for anthropic, openai, and zai providers; other providers force api_key (W040).

sidecar.description: When true, bring up a sidecar container for this provider (e.g. ollama).

sidecar_mcp_url.description: MCP SSE URL for external browser sidecar.

sidecar_cdp_host.description: CDP host for external browser sidecar.

sidecar_cdp_port.description: CDP port for external browser sidecar.

comfyui_builtin.description: Install ComfyUI as a built-in supervisor service on localhost:8188. Mutually exclusive with integrations.comfyui_external.enabled (E007).

enabled.description: VisionClaw ontology bridge — OWL2 DL class lookup, knowledge graph query, SPARQL (ADR-023).

visionclaw_api_url.description: VisionClaw REST API base URL (default: http://visionclaw-server:4000).

direct_axiom_load.description: DANGER: enable the ungoverned ontology_axiom_add backdoor (POST /api/ontology/load), bypassing Whelk consistency + human approval. Off by default; set true only for admin/bootstrap.

condense.description: Offline ontology condensation (PRD-020 WS-2). A cheap LOCAL LLM summarises every knowledge-graph class into one retrieval sentence + synonyms, enriching the search-optimisation cache. OPERATOR INFRASTRUCTURE — leave disabled unless you run your own model endpoint. The endpoint is never baked with secrets; it is a plain LAN URL.

enabled.description: Enable the LLM condensation pass. When off, ontology search falls back to deterministic term extraction only (no synonym enrichment). Vanilla default: off.

endpoint.description: Base URL of your OpenAI-compatible or ollama LLM endpoint, e.g. http://192.168.2.48:8084/v1 (DiffusionGemma) or http://ollama:11434. Leave blank when disabled.

model.description: Model id to request, e.g. diffusiongemma-26B-A4B-it-Q8_0. Optional for single-model servers.

n_blocks.description: DiffusionGemma answer sizing (256 tokens per block). Ignored by ollama and autoregressive servers. 3 gives clean class summaries.

max_concurrency.description: Parallel in-flight requests. MUST be 1 for a single-context diffusion server (DiffusionGemma serialises internally; fan-out only queues). Raise only for a true multi-worker endpoint.

open_design.description: Structured intake pipeline, brand specs, dimensional critique gate.

precedent.description: Governance precedent system for promoted decisions.

gateway_url.description: URL of the email search gateway.

payment_router.description: Payment-router skill gate. When enabled, the management-api registers the /v1/pay/route endpoint and the spend-policy middleware is applied to all payment-gated routes. Requires payments.consumer.enabled=true (E-PAY3).

enabled.description: Enable the payment-router skill. Requires payments.consumer.enabled=true (E-PAY3 will fire otherwise).

conninfo.description: PostgreSQL DSN for external RuVector. Required when adapters.memory=external-pg (E002).

manage_sidecar.description: When true, compose owns the ruvector-postgres sidecar container.

image.description: Docker image for the managed sidecar.

data_volume.description: Named Docker volume holding the production dataset.

solid_pod_rs.description: First-party Rust Solid Protocol 0.11 server (ADR-010). Consumed by the pods adapter when adapters.pods=local-solid-rs.

enable_did_nostr.description: did:nostr resolver — maps did:nostr:<pubkey> (BIP-340 x-only hex) to Solid WebIDs with alsoKnownAs cross-verification (Sprint 6 upstream).

enable_webhook_signing.description: RFC 9421 Ed25519 signing of outbound Solid Notification webhooks (Sprint 6).

enable_quota.description: Per-pod storage quota via .quota.json sidecar (Sprint 8 P0).

quota_default_bytes.description: Default per-pod storage ceiling when enable_quota=true.

allowed_origins.description: CORS allowlist for native pod mesh (comma-separated).

admin_key_env.description: Env var name holding the admin provisioning PSK.

sign_requests.description: Originate signed NIP-98 from the pods adapter.

enable_mcp.description: Enable MCP 2025-03-26 tool surface at POST /mcp.

comfyui_external.description: Point agentbox at a pre-existing ComfyUI instance. Mutually exclusive with skills.media.comfyui_builtin (E007).

enabled.description: When true, skip the built-in supervisor block and inject COMFYUI_URL / COMFYUI_WS_URL env vars.

url.description: HTTP base URL of the external ComfyUI instance.

ws_url.description: WebSocket URL of the external ComfyUI instance.

consultants.description: Consultant-tier MCP servers (PRD-005 / ADR-011). Each enabled consultant runs as a stdio-transport MCP server registered under skills/mcp.json so the Claude / ruflo coordinator can invoke it via the consult/health/cost_estimate tools.

enabled.description: Master gate. Setting any sub-consultant to enabled=true requires this to be true (E036).

intelligence_signal.description: When true, every successful consultation also writes an ADR-043 QualitySignal file to /workspace/profiles/<stack>/intelligence/data/.

codex.description: OpenAI Codex Rust CLI subprocess consultant. Requires providers.openai (E035) and toolchains.codex (E037).

home.description: CODEX_HOME path under the user-isolated openai-user account.

antigravity.description: Google Antigravity CLI (agy) subprocess consultant. Requires providers.gemini (E035) and toolchains.antigravity_cli (E037).

model.description: Gemini model used by the Antigravity CLI subprocess. gemini-3.5-flash is the current recommended default.

zai.description: Z.AI / GLM via Anthropic-compatible claude-zai subprocess. Requires providers.zai (E035).

model.description: Z.AI / GLM model identifier. glm-5 is superseded by glm-5.2; glm-5.2 is the current recommended default.

perplexity.description: Perplexity HTTPS API consultant. Requires providers.perplexity (E035).

deepseek.description: DeepSeek HTTPS API consultant. Requires providers.deepseek (E035).

model.description: DeepSeek model identifier. deepseek-reasoner is retired — use deepseek-v4-0324.

gemini.description: Google Gemini API consultant. Requires providers.gemini (E035) and toolchains.gemini_cli (E037).

model.description: Gemini model identifier. gemini-2.5-pro is the previous default; gemini-3.5-flash is the current recommended default.

privacy_filter.description: Local PII redaction sidecar (openai/privacy-filter). Routing contract: docs/reference/adr/ADR-008-privacy-filter-routing.md.

enabled.description: Enable the local openai/privacy-filter sidecar and adapter middleware.

mode.description: off disables the sidecar; local-gpu requires a CUDA/ROCm backend (E023); local-cpu runs BF16 on CPU.

port.description: Loopback port for the opf-router sidecar. Default 9092.

policy.description: Per-adapter-slot redaction policy. Values: strict | soft | off (E025).

enabled.description: Entity classes to redact. Empty = all eight.

sovereign_mesh.description: Sovereign-mesh subsystem (boundary: ADR-030) — Nostr identity, NIP-98 auth, inter-agent messaging, and the mobile agent bridge, gated as one subsystem (default off; env var remains the runtime override per the R7 fix). The phone↔agent path is pure Nostr (an Android Amethyst+Amber client talks to the embedded relay below); the device holds its own key plus a NIP-26 delegation from [operator], so no private key ships to the phone. Inbound NIP-59 gift wraps (kind 1059) are unwrapped by services/nostr-pod-bridge and persisted to the pod inbox; the durable conversation record is a kind-30840 session-summary dual-written to the relay and the pod. The whole mesh makes exactly one external data hop — the mobile_bridge Z.AI summarisation (ADR-030 D3); the per-turn live mirror (ADR-029) has none. This replaced the retired Telegram/CTM mirror.

voice_intent.description: WS7 (PRD-014 Seam B): bind a voice transcript to an agent intent and emit the canonical agent_action notification at POST /v1/voice-intent. Off → 503. Default false.

kg_elevation.description: WS6 (PRD-014 Seam D): scan the personal KG for elevation candidates and return GOVERNED ontology-propose descriptors at POST /v1/kg-elevation/scan. Off → 503. Default false.

junkiejarvis.description: JunkieJarvis forum agent — rides the management-api process and answers forum mentions over Nostr. Requires BOTH this gate AND the JUNKIEJARVIS_ENABLED=true env var; the env var remains the runtime override, so deployments without a manifest keep working. Default false.

per_user_agents.description: Per-user agent sessions (per-user-agent.js + run-per-user-agent.cjs): one Claude session per pod user, spawned on inbound relay messages. Canonical gate for ADR-028 (Per-User Agent Fabric, Accepted) — this key, [sovereign_mesh].per_user_agents, is the gate, NOT a top-level [per_user_agents] table (see ADR-028 gate-key alignment + ADR-030). Default false.

operator.description: Operator (human owner) Nostr identity. Its pubkey is added to the relay allowlist, granted NIP-98 operator access, used as the WebID owner in pod ACLs, and — for the mobile bridge — tagged as the NIP-26 delegator: the phone signs with its own key under a delegation from this pubkey, so no operator private key ever ships to the device. Private key is never stored here; pass it via OPERATOR_NOSTR_PRIVKEY or NIP-07/NIP-46 remote signing.

relay.description: Embedded Nostr relay — the ingress point for the mobile agent bridge. An Android Amethyst+Amber client connects here over NIP-01; signature-verified events addressed to a local npub are unwrapped (NIP-59 gift wraps) by services/nostr-pod-bridge and written to the pod inbox. See ADR-009 and PRD-004.

enabled.description: Turn the embedded relay on. Required for the mobile bridge, inter-agent messaging, and mesh federation.

implementation.description: Relay backend. nostr-rs-relay is the bundled plain relay; the Nix-built nostr-pod-bridge binary wraps it with NIP-59 unwrap + pod-inbox writes for the mobile path. rnostr was dropped from the enum because no flake supervisor branch wires it up.

bind.description: Loopback or 0.0.0.0. See E029. Keep loopback for standalone; the phone reaches it via the HTTPS/Tailscale bridge, not a raw exposed port.

ingress_policy.description: Who may publish. 'allowlist' (default) accepts only allowed_pubkeys (+ own npub); the phone's delegated pubkey is accepted via its NIP-26 tag from the operator. 'signed-only' accepts any valid signature; 'open' disables NIP-42 AUTH.

allowed_pubkeys.description: Hex pubkeys permitted to publish under 'allowlist'. Empty = self only; the operator pubkey is auto-added at boot. Add the phone's pubkey here for direct (non-delegated) mobile access.

allowed_kinds.description: Event kinds the relay accepts. Mobile bridge essentials: 1059 (NIP-59 gift wrap, the encrypted phone→agent envelope) and 30840 (the addressable session-summary that is the durable conversation record dual-written to relay + pod).

pod_bridge.description: When true, every verified event targeting a local npub is persisted to pods/<npub>/events/inbox/<id>.json — the durable mailbox the agent reads. This is what makes the mobile bridge survive a relay restart.

external_fanout.description: Outbound replication to the forum_relay_url / NOSTR_RELAYS fan-out list. 'off' keeps the relay loopback-only (matches federation standalone).

allow_nip04.description: Legacy unencrypted DMs. Warned by E031.

forum_relay_url.description: Private relay URL for governance event exchange with the forum.

mobile_bridge.description: Egress half of the mobile bridge: mirror a curated session digest (summary + actions + actionable questions, NOT the full transcript) to the operator's phone. On SessionEnd, a hook distils the transcript via the paid Z.AI/GLM consultant (ADR-011) and pipes the digest into `nostr-pod-bridge summarise`, which signs a kind-30840 and dual-writes it to the relay + pod. When enabled=false the SessionEnd hook is not registered (no transcript leaves the box). Replaces the retired Telegram/CTM mirror.

enabled.description: Register the nostr-session-summary SessionEnd hook. Still no-ops at runtime unless the bridge secrets (AGENTBOX_BRIDGE_SK / AGENTBOX_BRIDGE_RECIPIENT_PUBKEY / AGENTBOX_POD_ROOT / AGENTBOX_ADMIN_PUBKEY) and a Z.AI key are present. Default false. Privacy note: when true, the transcript is sent to the Z.AI endpoint for summarisation — the one external hop on this path.

summary_model.description: Z.AI / GLM model used to summarise the session (exported as AGENTBOX_ZAI_MODEL to the hook). Default glm-5.2. Point ZAI_URL at a local GLM endpoint to keep summarisation on-box.

multi_user.description: Multi-tenant did:nostr per-user pods. See ADR-017 / PRD-007.

enabled.description: When true, agentbox hosts per-user pods at pods/<pubkey-hex>/. Default false (single-tenant).

provisioning_policy.description: Trust model for accepting new user pubkeys. See E055/E056/E057/W058.

invite_validity_seconds.description: Maximum invite validity window in seconds.

max_users.description: Safety cap on number of provisioned pods. Required > 0 when provisioning_policy='open' (E057).

suspend_inactive_after_days.description: Admin policy: pods inactive for N days are auto-suspended. 0 disables.

admin_pubkeys.description: Pubkeys (hex or npub) granted admin role over user pods. Operator pubkey auto-added at boot.

git.description: Git-versioned pods. When enabled, each pod directory is git-initted at provisioning time (solid-pod-rs alpha.12, JSS #466/#469/#471). See docs/user/git-pods.md.

enabled.description: Auto-init pods as git repos at provisioning time.

default_branch.description: Initial branch name. JSS #471 pins this to 'main'.

http_backend.description: Path or name of the git-http-backend CGI binary.

http_route_prefix.description: Management-API route prefix for pod git HTTP (must start with '/').

max_push_mb.description: Per-push body size limit in MB.

auth_mode.description: Auth mode for git operations. 'nip98' requires a NIP-98 bearer token.

read_public.description: Allow unauthenticated clone of pods (upload-pack). Default false.

security.description: Security hardening configuration. The baseline is always applied; exceptions add minimum necessary privilege for specific features.

audit_acknowledged.description: Operator acknowledgement of residual risk from security exceptions.

exceptions.description: Feature-keyed privilege exceptions. Each key must correspond to an enabled feature gate (E020).

desktop.description: Extra tmpfs mounts for Hyprland/wayvnc. Enabled when desktop.enabled=true.

tmpfs.description: Additional tmpfs mount paths beyond the hardened baseline.

gpu-rocm.description: ROCm device access. Enabled when gpu.backend=ollama-rocm.

gpu-cuda.description: NVIDIA runtime. Enabled when gpu.backend=ollama-cuda or local-cuda.

writable_volumes.description: Named volume mount strings for CUDA-specific writable paths.

inherits.description: Exception names whose deltas are merged first.

playwright.description: Chromium sandbox capability. Enabled when skills.browser.playwright=true.

cap_add.description: Linux capabilities to add (minimum: SYS_ADMIN for Chromium sandbox).

code-server.description: code-server config volume. Enabled when toolchains.code_server=true.

writable_volumes.description: Named volume mount strings appended to the service volumes list.

nostr-relay.description: Embedded Nostr relay data volume. Enabled when sovereign_mesh.relay.enabled=true.

writable_volumes.description: Named volume mount strings for the SQLite database and WAL.

solid-pod-rs.description: solid-pod-rs filesystem backend volume. Enabled when adapters.pods=local-solid-rs (ADR-010).

writable_volumes.description: Named volume mount strings for the .meta/.acl sidecars and atomic-rename temp files.

tailscale.description: Tailscale userspace networking. Enabled when networking.tailscale=true.

cap_add.description: Linux capabilities to add (NET_ADMIN for userspace tun).

writable_volumes.description: Named volume mount strings for durable node state.

consultants.description: Consultant tier audit log volume. Enabled when consultants.enabled=true.

writable_volumes.description: Named volume mount strings for JSONL audit logs.

mesh.description: Federation mesh configuration (PRD-010 Phase 3). Replaces the deprecated [federation] section.

mode.description: Deployment shape: standalone (all local fallbacks) or client (federated with host mesh).

external_url.description: Base URL of the host mesh. Required when mode=client.

peer_urls.description: List of peer mesh URLs for bidirectional federation.

auth_mode.description: Authentication mode for mesh federation. Default nip98.

sync_interval_sec.description: How often (seconds) this node syncs state with the host mesh. Only applies when mode=client.

max_hops.description: Maximum relay hops for routed mesh messages.

tailscale.description: Enable Tailscale userspace networking. WARNING: bypasses did:nostr identity boundary.

hostname.description: MagicDNS hostname on the tailnet. Must be unique per instance.

host_gateway.description: Gate /etc/hosts host-gateway alias (host.docker.internal). Default false for air-gapped deployments.

llm_marketplace.description: LLM Resource Marketplace (kinds 38300-38305). Mesh-wide negotiation of LLM compute resources between did:nostr identities.

enabled.description: Enable the LLM marketplace subsystem.

auto_advertise.description: Auto-publish kind-38300 capability events for available models on relay startup.

max_grant_ttl.description: Maximum grant TTL in seconds. Grants exceeding this are clamped.

max_tokens_per_grant.description: Maximum token allocation per grant. 0 = unlimited.

pricing_mode.description: operator = operator-set pricing; market = accept mesh best-offer.

admin_access_mode.description: permissive = admin sees all namespaces; scoped = all callers restricted to their own pubkey namespace.

expel_lesson_extraction.description: Post-task experiential learning. Distils lessons per completed trajectory into RuVector (ADR-019).

enabled.description: Enable ExpeL lesson extraction after task completion.

max_lessons_per_task.description: Cap on lessons extracted per trajectory.

min_confidence.description: Minimum confidence to store a lesson.

archive_after_days.description: Lessons below floor demoted to *-archive after this many days.

contradiction_sample_rate.description: Fraction of retrievals sampled for LLM-judge contradiction check.

payments.description: Payment system configuration (Web Ledger / DREAM token).

enabled.description: Enable the payment subsystem.

backend.description: Payment backend. solid-pod-rs proxies to local pod.

dream_per_sat.description: DREAM token exchange rate per satoshi.

hold_buffer_ratio.description: Hold buffer ratio for payment pre-auth.

consumer.description: Consumer-facing spend-policy enforced by the spend-policy middleware. All fields are read by management-api/middleware/spend-policy.js. enabled must be true for any inbound payment calls to be accepted (fail-closed).

enabled.description: Master gate. false or absent causes every call to be rejected with HTTP 402 consumer-payments-disabled.

max_sats_per_call.description: Hard per-call satoshi cap. Requests whose cost exceeds this value are rejected with HTTP 402 exceeds-per-call-cap.

daily_budget_sats.description: Rolling daily satoshi budget per origin. Accumulated across the process lifetime (in-memory, resets on restart). Requests that would push spend over this limit are rejected with HTTP 402 daily-budget-exceeded.

approval_threshold_sats.description: Calls whose cost exceeds this threshold have request.requiresApproval=true set. The call is NOT denied — downstream handlers may choose to gate or log it.

allow_origins.description: Origin allowlist. When non-empty, only listed origins may spend. Requests from unlisted origins are rejected with HTTP 402 origin-not-allowed. Empty list means any origin is allowed.

deny_origins.description: Origin denylist. Checked before allow_origins. Requests from a listed origin are rejected with HTTP 402 origin-denied.

external_router.description: External payment-router integration. off disables any outbound routing; cinderwright enables the Cinderwright Lightning router sidecar.

broadcast.description: Payment broadcast configuration — controls whether this agentbox advertises payment capabilities on the mesh.

enabled.description: Enable outbound payment-capability broadcast.

well_known.description: Serve a /.well-known/payments.json capability document. Requires broadcast.enabled=true (W-PAY1 when enabled=false).

accepts_block.description: Declare that this node accepts block-level payment settlements.

health_signals.description: Emit periodic payment-health signals on the mesh event bus.

index_submit.description: Payment index submission mode. Only 'off' is currently implemented.

compression.description: Content-aware compression for agent context windows and storage (PRD-016, ADR-034). Uses in-process Rust N-API bindings (headroom-napi crate).

enabled.description: Build headroom-napi crate and activate compression middleware.

linked_data.description: Linked-Data interchange surfaces (PRD-006 / ADR-012 / DDD-004). When [linked_data].enabled = false (default), no surface emits or consumes JSON-LD and zero behaviour changes vs. the pre-PRD-006 product. Each per-surface gate accepts on | emit | off. Validation rules: E040–E049.

enabled.description: Master gate. When false every per-surface gate is forced off (E040).

pods.description: S1 — Solid pod resource representations. Requires adapters.pods ∈ {local-solid-rs, external} (E041).

events.description: S2 — Nostr envelope payloads. Requires [sovereign_mesh.relay].enabled = true (E042).

credentials.description: S3 — W3C Verifiable Credentials. JCS canonicalisation required (E043).

did_documents.description: S4 — DID Documents at /.well-known/did.json. Requires sovereign_mesh.solid_pod = true (E044).

provenance.description: S5 — PROV-O receipts emitted parallel to the events adapter.

capability_descriptors.description: S6 — W3C WoT Thing Descriptions for MCP servers.

skill_metadata.description: S7 — Schema.org HowTo encoding for skills (build-time only).

payments.description: S8 — agentic-payment mandates and receipts (VC + ODRL). JCS canonicalisation required (E043).

memory_catalogue.description: S9 — DCAT-3 memory namespace catalogue at /v1/memory/catalogue.

default_form.description: Default JSON-LD form when a surface does not pin one. Default compacted.

context_catalogue.description: Filesystem path of the pinned context catalogue. Default /opt/agentbox/contexts.

context_cache_mode.description: How the catalogue reaches the runtime. image (read-only image layer; default), bind (host bind-mount), off (rejected when any user-touching surface is on; E046).

unknown_context_policy.description: What to do when an UnpinnedContextIRI appears in input. Default fail-closed (E047 warns when fail-open is paired with pods=on).

canonicalisation.description: Canonicalisation mode. jcs is required when credentials or payments surface is enabled (E043).

round_trip_in_dispatch.description: Run the round-trip self-check on every dispatch. Default true. CI-only deployments may set false to save cycles.

contexts.description: Operator overrides for context IRIs. Each value MUST resolve to a pinned ContextDocument in lib/linked-data-contexts.nix (E045).

method.description: DID method. nostr is the default sovereign method; requires the did-nostr Cargo feature (E049).

service_endpoints.description: Which agentbox services to publish in the DID Document service array.

publish_to_well_known.description: Serve the DID Document at /.well-known/did.json via solid-pod-rs.

privacy_handoff.description: Privacy filter handoff (documentation only — the order is fixed in code).

order.description: Always after. The validator rejects any other value (E048).

viewer.description: Linked-Object Viewer slot (S12, PRD-006 §15). Mounts a JSON-LD-aware browser at /lo/* that renders every PRD-006 surface. Pane manifest at /lo/manifest.json. Default off. Validation rules: E050–E054, W053.

mode.description: off (default), local-linkedobjects (bundle linkedobjects/browser into the image at /opt/agentbox/browser; AGPL-3.0), external (operator-supplied URL).

mount_path.description: URL prefix for the viewer surface. Default /lo. Must start with / and contain no trailing slash.

bundle_path.description: Override for the on-disk bundle root. Default /opt/agentbox/browser. Used when mode = local-linkedobjects.

external_url.description: External viewer URL when mode = external (E051).

sri_hash.description: Optional Subresource Integrity hash for external bundles. SHA-{256,384,512}-base64 form (E052).

expose_port.description: Whether /lo/* is reachable from outside the host. Default false (loopback only).

extra_panes.description: Operator-supplied pane URLs or filesystem paths layered on top of built-in + upstream panes.

upstream_panes_visible.description: Show upstream linkedobjects/browser panes in the manifest. Default true.

source_code_header.description: Override for the Source-Code response header. Default = upstream repo URL (AGPL §13 compliance).
